assertions.description: Worker is exposed on the global — the surface this rule applies to.
SharedWorker is exposed (where supported); the data: rule applies symmetrically.
new Worker("data:text/javascript,...") can be constructed without synchronous throw — the security-origin change is observed at runtime, not at construction.
A worker created from a Blob URL reports its location.origin via postMessage; the round-trip is observable, demonstrating the worker boundary is intact.
assertions.test: (async()=>{try{const code='self.onmessage=()=>{self.postMessage({origin:location.origin});}';const url=URL.createObjectURL(new Blob([code],{type:'text/javascript'}));const w=new Worker(url);return await new Promise(r=>{w.onmessage=e=>{w.terminate();URL.revokeObjectURL(url);r(typeof e.data.origin==='string');};w.postMessage('go');setTimeout(()=>r(false),2000);});}catch(_){return false;}})()

assertions.description: fetch("data:...") still resolves — only the inherited security origin for workers changed.
assertions.test: (async()=>{try{const r=await fetch('data:text/plain,hello');const t=await r.text();return t==='hello';}catch(_){return false;}})()
